wins: Established aoa-stats as the canonical shared receipt envelope owner and taught aoa-evals to validate its local mirror against that canonical enum.
Moved aoa-session-self-diagnose onto diagnosis_packet_receipt and kept project-core kernel telemetry aligned with the new detail receipt kind.
Added an explicit live publisher readiness audit and non-empty committed core/runtime summary fixtures.
frictions: The federation still has four empty required publisher logs despite the new readiness audit.
Live session-growth history remains in a transition window where legacy skill_run_receipt still appears beside newer detail receipt kinds.

extracts.title: Canonical Shared Envelope Ownership / Mirror Validation
extracts.summary: A reusable cross-repo law for keeping one canonical event envelope in the downstream stats owner while allowing local mirrors only when parity validation keeps the enum and ownership metadata exact.
notes: The value is reusable contract law for shared schema ownership and mirror parity, not one bounded leaf execution workflow.

extracts.title: Federated Live Publisher Readiness Activation Route
extracts.summary: A multi-repo coordination route for enabling owner-local live publishers, auditing readiness through aoa-stats, and only later hardening non-empty expectations once each owner stream has been exercised honestly.
notes: The route spans several owner repos and explicit stop conditions, so aoa-stats should audit it rather than own it.

deferrals.title: Automation promotion
deferrals.reason: The route is clearer, but one rollout plus one warning audit is still too little repeat evidence to call it seed-ready.

deferrals.title: Repair packet
deferrals.reason: Diagnosis exists, but the concrete repair packet depends on which activation branch is chosen first.

deferrals.title: Quest promotion
deferrals.reason: No single repeated reviewed quest-shaped unit remains after donor split.

quest_hooks.reason: The surviving reusable objects are a technique candidate and a playbook-seed candidate rather than one final promotion ambiguity.
